# Pull Request

## Description

<!-- Summarize the change, why it's needed, and link the related issue. -->

Fixes # (issue)

## Type of Change

- [ ] `fix` — Bug fix (non-breaking)
- [ ] `feat` — New feature (non-breaking)
- [ ] `refactor` — Code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- [ ] `perf` — Performance improvement
- [ ] `docs` — Documentation update
- [ ] `test` — Adding or updating tests
- [ ] `build` / `ci` — Build system or CI/CD changes
- [ ] `chore` — Maintenance (dependencies, configs, etc.)
- [ ] Breaking change (will cause existing functionality to not work as expected)

## Scope

<!-- Check all areas affected by this PR. -->

- [ ] Frontend
- [ ] Backend
- [ ] Database / Migrations

## How Has This Been Tested?

<!-- Describe the tests you ran. Include steps to reproduce if manual. -->

<!-- If manually tested, say it: -->

## Checklist

- [ ] My commit messages follow [Conventional Commits](/.github/COMMIT_CONVENTION.md)
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings or lint errors
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing tests pass locally (`npm run test` / `pytest`)
- [ ] Any new environment variables are documented and added to `.env.example`
- [ ] Any database migrations have been tested on a clean database
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published in downstream modules
